channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Argentina stage remarkable comeback against Egypt in World Cup clash
item.description: In a thrilling World Cup match, Argentina delivered a stunning comeback against Egypt to secure their place in the next round. The defending champions faced a daunting deficit, trailing by two goals i...

item.title: Unstable Midtown High-Rise Building Evacuated After Structural Columns Buckle
item.description: A 33-story high-rise building in Midtown Manhattan was evacuated after structural columns buckled, prompting officials to describe the situation as 'extremely serious.' The building, formerly Pfizer's...

item.title: Cristiano Ronaldo's Final World Cup Appearance as Portugal Exit to Spain
item.description: Portugal's World Cup campaign ended with a Round of 16 exit to Spain, marking Cristiano Ronaldo's final appearance at the tournament. The five-time Ballon d'Or winner confirmed this was his last World...

item.title: Yas and ZTE Introduce Affordable High-Speed Internet Access in Tanzania
item.description: Tanzanian businesses, households, and institutions are set to gain improved access to high-speed internet following a new partnership between telecommunications provider Yas Tanzania and Chinese techn...

item.title: Government and industry clarify no issues with E20 fuel usage
item.description: Petroleum minister Hardeep Puri addressed concerns about E20 fuel, stating that automobile manufacturers and service providers have no issues with the blend. He emphasized that the government is not r...

item.title: Renata Ford, Wife of Late Toronto Mayor Rob Ford, Passes Away; Family Reeling from Loss
item.description: Renata Ford, the wife of former Toronto mayor Rob Ford, has passed away at the age of 55. Ontario Premier Doug Ford expressed deep sorrow for her children, Stephanie and Douglas, stating that their lo...

item.title: NBI Introduces Digital eClearance Service for Efficient Public Access
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has launched a digital eClearance service through the eGovPH Super App, streamlining the process for citizens to obtain clearance. This initiative, integrate...

item.title: Argentina Advances to World Cup Quarterfinals with Late Comeback Against Egypt
item.description: Argentina secured a dramatic 3-2 victory over Egypt in their FIFA World Cup 2026 clash, advancing to the quarterfinals. The match, held at Atlanta Stadium, saw Egypt take an early 2-0 lead through Yas...

item.title: Zanzibar Resort Lease Dispute Sparks Concerns Over Investor Treatment
item.description: A British developer, Opulent, has accused Zanzibar authorities of unlawfully revoking a 99-year lease for its planned Pongwe resort without proper legal procedures. This dispute has sparked debates ab...

item.title: US and Philippines Condemn China's Missile Test as Threat to Regional Stability
item.description: The United States has expressed strong support for the Philippines following concerns over China's recent ballistic missile test, which the US and Philippine officials argue undermines regional stabil...

item.title: E's rise to icon status following his 'glow-up' era in Naples and his Italian fashion influence
item.description: The article discusses the meteoric rise of an individual named E, who has become a cultural icon after a transformative period known as his 'glow-up' era in Naples. This phase of personal development ...

item.title: Four Suspects Face Animal Cruelty and Pornography Charges Following Police Investigation in Latvia
item.description: Latvian State Police have detained four individuals suspected of systematically abusing animals over several years at a stable near Riga. According to investigators, the alleged offenses took place fr...

item.title: Messi's Argentina Advances in World Cup Match Against Egypt
item.description: Lionel Messi led Argentina to a crucial World Cup victory against Egypt, marking a significant moment in the tournament. The match, played on July 8, 2026, saw Argentina overcome challenges to secure ...

item.title: Egypt Advances Past Argentina in FIFA World Cup Round of 16
item.description: In a thrilling FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16 clash, Egypt stunned Argentina with a 2-1 victory at Atlanta Stadium. Despite Lionel Messi's efforts, including a missed penalty and a shot hitting the p...

item.title: Ottawa invests $400 million in Teck’s B.C. smelter to secure critical minerals
item.description: The Canadian government has announced a $400-million investment into Teck Resources Ltd.’s Trail smelter in British Columbia through the Canada Critical Minerals Accelerator (CCMA), part of its broade...

item.title: Tony Dokoupil's Career and Financial Overview
item.description: The article provides an in-depth look at Tony Dokoupil's professional journey, financial status, and personal life. Dokoupil, a prominent broadcast journalist, transitioned from magazine journalism to...

item.title: Gauteng Government and Lesedi Municipality Collaborate to Stabilize Water Supply Amid Protests
item.description: The Gauteng government has announced a key agreement to address ongoing water supply challenges in the Lesedi local municipality following recent protests. Residents in Ratanda faced weeks without wat...

item.title: Ancient Australian rocks shed light on Earth's early water recycling processes
item.description: A groundbreaking study of 3.1 billion-year-old rocks in Western Australia's Pilbara Craton has uncovered evidence of an ancient water cycle operating before modern plate tectonics. Researchers from Ad...

item.title: Former IT installer seeks R5m compensation after RAF negligence
item.description: A former IT installer, Kgaugelo Kaditsi, has attached several Road Accident Fund (RAF) assets worth over R5m to settle a compensation claim following a 2019 car accident. The Pretoria High Court order...

item.title: Canada selects European submarine deal over South Korea amid NATO discussions
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney met with South Korean President Lee Jae Myung at the NATO summit following Canada's decision to choose Germany and Norway's TKMS submarine deal over South Korea's Hanwha Oce...
